source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Personnel Basket Incident at WD 73 C

event.top_event: Personnel Fall from Basket During Transfer
actions_taken: Injured person evaluated by platform medic
Medical evaluation at OMS clinic in Houma LA
Incident reported to BSEE New Orleans District
Safety Alert Bulletin issued by Cox dated June 20, 2022
event.summary: On 19 June 2022 at 0930 hours at West Delta 73 C, during personnel transfer from Motor Vessel Sapphire to platform via 8-man personnel basket, the basket made contact with a scrap iron basket on the vessel deck. One of eight personnel fell 2-3 feet from the basket to the deck, sustaining a fractured right heel. The incident resulted from the crane operator lifting the basket prematurely without proper signaling while the vessel was drifting, combined with inadequate hazard identification in the Job Safety Analysis and a congested vessel deck.
recommendations: Ensure all personnel involved with personnel transfers are familiar with and discuss all potential hazards affecting safe outcome
Use JSA, JSEA, Hazard Analysis, and pre-job safety meetings to communicate hazards to everyone
Identify vessel underpowering and engine issues in shared JSA documentation
Implement Stop Work Authority procedures to halt unsafe operations

threats.name: Vessel drifting away from platform during personnel transfer
Crane operator lifting basket without proper signaling or tagline security
Personnel basket dragging across vessel deck
Collision with obstructions on vessel deck
Vessel engine failure reducing positioning capability
Communication breakdown between crane operator and vessel personnel

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and communicate safe procedures

supporting_text: BSEE has determined that all possible hazards associated with the transferring of personnel from the motor vessel to the platform were not identified and shared with personnel involved in the lift through the JSA process
The JSA was not signed by any of the M/V Sapphire personnel
The M/V being underpowered should have been listed on a shared JSA to be discussed and provide methods to prevent an undesirable event

controls.name: Crane Operator Signaling Protocol
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper communication before lifting personnel basket
controls.lod_basis: Standard lifting procedures requiring deckhand signal before lift

supporting_text: The crane operator made the decision to immediately lift or hoist up the basket, even though the deckhand had not given the lift-up signal or secured the tag line in hand
the crane operator should not have lifted the basket without proper signaling from the deckhand/rigger

controls.name: Tagline Control and Security
controls.barrier_role: Secure and control basket movement during transfer
controls.lod_basis: Standard rigging practice requiring tagline security before lift

supporting_text: the deckhand had not given the lift-up signal or secured the tag line in hand
The quick action taken by the deckhand enabled him to untangle the tag line and free the IP's leg

controls.name: Vessel Positioning and Holding
controls.barrier_role: Maintain vessel position under crane boom during transfer
controls.lod_basis: Vessel engine capability and captain positioning control

supporting_text: the M/V began drifting away from the platform
The boat captain was having a difficult time keeping the boat centered under the crane boom tip
The M/V Sapphire was running only on one main engine per the witness statement submitted by the deckhand
prior to or during the lift, the boat had engine problems but did not communicate these issues with platform personnel

controls.name: Communication Systems (Handheld Radios)
controls.barrier_role: Enable communication between crane operator and vessel captain
controls.lod_basis: Radio communication between crane operator and M/V captain

supporting_text: there were communication problems with the handheld radios used to communicate between the crane operator and M/V captain
Crane operator handheld radio not functional

controls.name: Hand Signal Communication (Crane Operator to Deckhand)
controls.barrier_role: Communicate lifting instructions between crane operator and deckhand
controls.lod_basis: Standard hand signal protocol for crane operations

supporting_text: between the crane operator and the deckhand/rigger – hand signals
The deckhand was attempting to tell the 8 riders 'to get off the basket' just as the crane operator was starting to pick up

controls.name: Deck Workspace Organization and Clearance
controls.barrier_role: Maintain clear landing and lifting area for personnel basket
controls.lod_basis: Safe work environment with adequate space for personnel transfer operations

supporting_text: the deck was crowded with equipment, tool boxes, baskets, a welding machine, and bottle racks, which left a small narrow area for landing and lifting the large 8-man personnel basket
The landing area for the personnel basket did not have sufficient space for the large 8-man personnel basket
BSEE also determined that the crowded back deck of the M/V Sapphire was a contributing factor

controls.name: Personnel Hazard Awareness and Stop Work Authority
controls.barrier_role: Enable personnel to identify unsafe conditions and halt operations
controls.lod_basis: Personnel awareness and authority to stop unsafe work

supporting_text: several personnel basket riders had concerns prior to the lift but did not voice these concerns
several riders put in their statements that they could tell that 'the crane boom tip was not centered' before the lift started
BSEE agrees with Cox's assessment that there were several opportunities for multiple personnel to use Stop Work Authority

controls.name: Medical Response and Evacuation
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate medical care and evacuation for injured personnel
controls.lod_basis: On-site medical capability and helicopter evacuation procedures

supporting_text: The Injured Person (IP) was seen by a medic and then evacuated by helicopter for further medical evaluation by a physician onshore
The platform medic was lowered back down on to the M/V to provide medical assistance

controls.name: Alternative Personnel Transfer Method
controls.barrier_role: Provide alternative safe transfer method when basket transfer is unsafe
controls.lod_basis: Alternative boat landing area available at platform

supporting_text: There was a +10 boat landing area available at WD 73 C in the event personnel could not transfer by personnel basket

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
